diagrams.title: OS Definition and Role
diagrams.description: This diagram illustrates the operating system as an intermediary layer between hardware and user applications. It shows the OS serving dual roles: as a resource manager (controlling CPU, memory, I/O devices) and as a user interface (command-line or GUI). This helps students understand what an OS actually is and its fundamental purpose in computing.
elements: Labels for 'User Interface' and 'Resource Manager' roles
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental definition of an operating system and its position in the system hierarchy

diagrams.title: OS Structure - Layered Architecture
diagrams.description: This diagram shows the internal structure of an operating system using a layered approach. It depicts the kernel at the core, surrounded by system services, API layer, and user interface. This visual representation helps students understand how OS components are organized and interact with each other.
elements: System Services (file system, device drivers, security)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the internal organization and layered structure of modern operating systems

diagrams.title: OS Main Operations and Subsystems
diagrams.description: This diagram presents the major operations performed by an operating system. It shows the key subsystems: Process Management, Memory Management, File Management, I/O Management, and Security/Protection. Each subsystem box contains its primary functions, helping students understand the scope of OS operations.
elements: Memory Management box (allocation, virtual memory, paging)
File Management box (directories, file operations, storage)
I/O Management box (device drivers, buffering, caching)
diagrams.purpose: Explains the five major operations/functions that an operating system performs to manage system resources
